name: get_request_always_redirects
code:
proof fn phi_1_get_request_always_redirects(
    pre: AbstractHostState, post: AbstractHostState,
    src: AbstractEndPoint, seqno: nat, k: AbstractKey,
    sm: SingleMessage<Message>, m: Message, out: Set<Packet>, should_send: bool
)
    requires
        pre.delegation_map.is_complete(),
        next_get_request_reply(pre, post, src, seqno, k, sm, m, out, should_send),
        should_send,
        valid_key(k),
    ensures
        m is Redirect,
{
}
reason: Would mean the host can never serve a GetRequest locally, always redirecting even for owned keys.

name: set_request_no_hashtable_mutation
code:
proof fn phi_2_set_request_no_hashtable_mutation(
    pre: AbstractHostState, post: AbstractHostState,
    src: AbstractEndPoint, seqno: nat, reqm: Message,
    sm: SingleMessage<Message>, replym: Message, out: Set<Packet>, should_send: bool
)
    requires
        pre.delegation_map.is_complete(),
        reqm is SetRequest,
        next_set_request_complete(pre, post, src, seqno, reqm, sm, replym, out, should_send),
    ensures
        post.h == pre.h,
{
}
reason: Would mean SetRequests never actually modify the hashtable, rendering writes completely inert.

name: process_message_no_requests_logged
reason: Would mean the host never appends to received_requests, breaking application-level request tracking.

name: shard_send_produces_empty_output
code:
proof fn phi_4_shard_send_produces_empty_output(
    pre: AbstractHostState, post: AbstractHostState, out: Set<Packet>,
    kr: KeyRange<AbstractKey>, recipient: AbstractEndPoint,
    sm: SingleMessage<Message>, should_send: bool
)
    requires
        pre.delegation_map.is_complete(),
        next_shard(pre, post, out, kr, recipient, sm, should_send),
        should_send,
    ensures
        out == Set::<Packet>::empty(),
{
}
reason: Would mean shard operations never transmit Delegate packets to recipients even when should_send is true.

name: delegate_no_delegation_count_increase
code:
proof fn phi_5_delegate_no_delegation_count_increase(
    pre: AbstractHostState, post: AbstractHostState, pkt: Packet, out: Set<Packet>
)
    requires
        pkt.msg is Message,
        pre.delegation_map.is_complete(),
        next_delegate(pre, post, pkt, out),
        pre.constants.host_ids.contains(pkt.src),
    ensures
        post.num_delegations == pre.num_delegations,
{
}
reason: Would mean accepted Delegate messages never increment the delegation counter, breaking the max-delegations safety bound.
